condition: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
description: A founder mutation in BRCA1, particularly common in Ashkenazi Jewish populations. Significantly increases lifetime risk of breast and ovarian cancer.
het_description: Carrier of one copy. Significantly elevated risk: 60-80% lifetime breast cancer risk, 40-60% ovarian cancer risk.
hom_description: Homozygous pathogenic — extremely rare. Very high cancer risk requiring immediate clinical management.

condition: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
description: One of three Ashkenazi Jewish founder mutations in BRCA1. Causes a frameshift leading to a truncated, nonfunctional protein.
het_description: Carrier. 60-80% lifetime breast cancer risk. Increased ovarian, pancreatic, and prostate cancer risk.
hom_description: Homozygous — extremely rare and associated with very high cancer risk.

condition: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
description: Ashkenazi Jewish founder mutation in BRCA2. Frameshift mutation leading to loss of BRCA2 tumor suppressor function.
het_description: Carrier. 45-70% lifetime breast cancer risk. Also increases risk of ovarian, pancreatic, and prostate cancer.
hom_description: Homozygous pathogenic — extremely rare. Associated with Fanconi anemia in biallelic state.

condition: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
description: Pathogenic frameshift variant in BRCA2 that results in premature protein truncation and loss of DNA repair function.
het_description: Carrier with significantly increased breast and ovarian cancer risk.
hom_description: Extremely rare; associated with Fanconi anemia subtype.

condition: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
description: Pathogenic BRCA1 frameshift mutation causing loss of tumor suppressor function. One of the well-characterized BRCA1 pathogenic variants.
het_description: Carrier with significantly elevated breast and ovarian cancer risk.
hom_description: Homozygous extremely rare. Associated with embryonic lethality or very severe phenotype.

description: One of two SNPs that define APOE isoforms. The C allele (with rs7412-C) defines the ε4 allele, the strongest common genetic risk factor for late-onset Alzheimer's disease.
het_description: One ε4 allele (if rs7412 is also C/C): 3-4x increased Alzheimer's risk. Earlier average age of onset.
hom_description: Two ε4 alleles (ε4/ε4): 8-15x increased Alzheimer's risk. Average onset ~68 years vs ~84 for ε3/ε3.

description: Second APOE-defining SNP. The T allele (with rs429358-C) defines ε4 (risk). The C allele (with rs429358-T) defines ε2, which is protective against Alzheimer's but associated with type III hyperlipoproteinemia.
het_description: One ε2 allele may offer moderate Alzheimer's protection. Check combined APOE genotype.
hom_description: ε2/ε2: strong Alzheimer's protection but ~5-10% risk of type III hyperlipoproteinemia.

description: The most common inherited thrombophilia. A single nucleotide change in the Factor V gene (G1691A) makes the protein resistant to inactivation by Protein C, leading to increased clotting risk.
het_description: Heterozygous carrier: 3-8x increased risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE). Avoid estrogen-containing contraceptives; discuss with physician before surgery.
hom_description: Homozygous: 50-80x increased VTE risk. Significant clinical implications. May require lifelong anticoagulation. Avoid all hormonal contraception.

description: Common variant reducing MTHFR enzyme activity to ~65% (het) or ~30% (hom) of normal. Affects folate metabolism and homocysteine levels.
het_description: One copy (CT): ~65% enzyme activity. Mildly elevated homocysteine. Ensure adequate folate intake.
hom_description: Two copies (TT): ~30% enzyme activity. Significantly elevated homocysteine. Increased risk of neural tube defects, cardiovascular events. Supplemental methylfolate recommended.

description: Second common MTHFR variant. Alone has mild effect; combined with C677T (compound heterozygote) can significantly reduce enzyme activity.
het_description: One copy: minimal clinical effect alone. Check for compound heterozygosity with C677T.
hom_description: Two copies: moderate reduction in MTHFR activity. Combined with C677T heterozygosity may significantly elevate homocysteine.

description: The primary mutation causing hereditary hemochromatosis type 1. Disrupts iron regulation leading to progressive iron overload that can damage the liver, heart, and pancreas.
het_description: Carrier. Usually no clinical disease alone. If combined with H63D (compound heterozygote), mild iron overload possible.
hom_description: Homozygous C282Y: ~80% risk of iron overload. Men more severely affected. Regular phlebotomy recommended. Monitor ferritin and transferrin saturation.

description: Second HFE variant associated with hereditary hemochromatosis. Alone causes milder iron loading than C282Y. Compound heterozygosity (C282Y/H63D) increases risk.
het_description: Carrier. Low risk of iron overload alone. Combined with C282Y increases risk significantly.
hom_description: Homozygous H63D: mildly elevated risk of iron overload. Monitor ferritin levels periodically.

description: Tag SNP for HLA-DQ2.5 haplotype, present in ~90-95% of celiac disease patients. Necessary but not sufficient for celiac disease development.
het_description: Heterozygous carrier of HLA-DQ2.5. Significantly increased risk of celiac disease (~3% lifetime risk vs 1% general population).
hom_description: Homozygous: highest genetic risk for celiac disease (~10% lifetime risk). Especially elevated risk if first-degree relative affected.

description: Strongest genetic risk factor at the 10q26 locus for age-related macular degeneration (AMD). Located in the ARMS2/HTRA1 region.
het_description: One risk allele: ~2.7x increased AMD risk. Recommend regular eye exams after age 50, protective eyewear, and antioxidant-rich diet.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~8x increased AMD risk. High-dose AREDS supplementation may be beneficial. Annual dilated eye exams strongly recommended.

description: First identified common variant for AMD risk. Complement factor H variant that alters inflammatory regulation in the retina.
het_description: One risk allele: ~2.5x increased AMD risk. Regular eye monitoring recommended.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~6x increased AMD risk. Combined with ARMS2 risk alleles can exceed 30x risk.

description: Z allele of alpha-1 antitrypsin (AAT). Causes protein misfolding and polymerization in the liver, leading to severe AAT deficiency, early-onset emphysema, and liver disease.
het_description: MZ carrier: AAT levels ~60% of normal. Mild increase in COPD risk, especially with smoking. Avoid smoking absolutely.
hom_description: ZZ genotype: AAT levels ~10-15% of normal. High risk of early-onset emphysema and liver cirrhosis. Eligible for AAT augmentation therapy.

description: The sickle cell mutation. A single amino acid substitution (Glu→Val) in beta-globin causes hemoglobin S polymerization under low-oxygen conditions.
het_description: Sickle cell trait (HbAS): generally asymptomatic carrier. Confers malaria resistance. Rare complications under extreme conditions (altitude, dehydration).
hom_description: Sickle cell disease (HbSS): chronic hemolytic anemia, vaso-occlusive crises, organ damage. Requires comprehensive medical management. Life expectancy reduced but improving with modern care.

description: Prothrombin gene mutation causing elevated prothrombin levels and increased risk of venous thromboembolism. Second most common inherited thrombophilia after Factor V Leiden.
het_description: Heterozygous: 2-5x increased VTE risk. Caution with hormonal contraceptives, prolonged immobility, and surgery.
hom_description: Homozygous: significantly elevated VTE risk. May warrant prophylactic anticoagulation in high-risk situations.

description: The most common cystic fibrosis mutation worldwide, accounting for ~70% of CF alleles. Deletion of phenylalanine at position 508 causes CFTR protein misfolding.
het_description: CF carrier. No symptoms. 1 in 25 Caucasians carry one copy. If partner is also a carrier, 25% risk of affected child.
hom_description: Cystic fibrosis. Severe disease affecting lungs, pancreas, and other organs. Eligible for CFTR modulator therapy (Trikafta).

description: Gating mutation in CFTR (~4% of CF alleles). CFTR protein reaches cell surface but channel does not open properly. Excellent response to ivacaftor (Kalydeco).
het_description: CF carrier. No symptoms. If compound heterozygous with another CF allele, causes cystic fibrosis.
hom_description: Cystic fibrosis. Responds well to ivacaftor therapy.

description: Nonsense mutation creating a premature stop codon. No functional CFTR protein produced. Common worldwide CF mutation (~3% of CF alleles).
het_description: CF carrier. No symptoms.
hom_description: Cystic fibrosis with severe phenotype due to complete absence of CFTR protein.

description: Common G6PD deficiency variant (A- type) prevalent in Africa. Reduces enzyme activity to ~10-20% of normal. Triggers hemolytic anemia with oxidative stressors.
het_description: Female heterozygote: variable expression due to X-inactivation. May have mild to moderate G6PD deficiency.
hom_description: Hemizygous male or homozygous female: G6PD deficiency. Avoid fava beans, certain drugs (primaquine, sulfonamides, dapsone, nitrofurantoin). Risk of hemolytic crises.

description: Mediterranean variant of G6PD deficiency. More severe than A- type, reducing enzyme activity to <5% of normal. Prevalent in Middle Eastern and Mediterranean populations.
het_description: Female heterozygote: variable expression. May experience hemolytic episodes.
hom_description: Hemizygous male or homozygous female: severe G6PD deficiency. Strict avoidance of oxidative triggers required.

description: Loss-of-function variant in PCSK9 that lowers LDL cholesterol by ~15-28%. Associated with significant reduction in coronary artery disease risk.
het_description: One copy: LDL cholesterol reduced by ~15-28%. Up to 88% reduction in coronary heart disease risk in African Americans, 47% in Caucasians.
hom_description: Two copies: very low LDL cholesterol. No adverse health effects observed.

description: Common variant near LDLR associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular disease risk. FH affects ~1 in 250 people.
het_description: One copy: moderately elevated LDL cholesterol. Increased cardiovascular risk. Statin therapy may be beneficial.
hom_description: Two copies: significantly elevated LDL cholesterol. Cardiovascular screening and lipid management recommended.

description: Variant in beta-2 adrenergic receptor affecting bronchodilator response. The Gly16 allele is associated with reduced response to albuterol and increased asthma severity.
het_description: Intermediate bronchodilator response. May need dose adjustment for beta-agonists.
hom_description: Reduced response to short-acting beta-agonists. Consider alternative asthma management strategies.

description: Variant in hepatic transporter OATP1B1 reducing statin uptake into the liver. Increases systemic statin exposure and risk of myopathy, particularly with simvastatin.
het_description: Intermediate statin transporter function. 4.5x increased risk of simvastatin myopathy. Consider lower dose or alternative statin.
hom_description: Poor statin transporter function. 16.9x increased simvastatin myopathy risk. Avoid simvastatin. Use pravastatin or rosuvastatin at reduced doses.

description: Variant near IL28B/IFNL3 gene strongly predicting response to interferon-based hepatitis C treatment and spontaneous HCV clearance.
het_description: CT genotype: intermediate response to interferon-based HCV therapy. ~50% SVR rate with pegIFN/RBV.
hom_description: TT genotype: poor response to interferon therapy (~25% SVR). CC genotype (favorable): ~80% SVR. Direct-acting antivirals now preferred regardless of genotype.

description: Common variant in PPARG affecting insulin sensitivity and adipogenesis. The Pro12 (C) allele is associated with increased type 2 diabetes risk.
het_description: One risk allele: modestly increased type 2 diabetes risk. Lifestyle modifications particularly beneficial.
hom_description: Pro12/Pro12: ~1.25x increased T2D risk. Weight management and physical activity strongly recommended.

description: Strongest common genetic risk factor for type 2 diabetes. TCF7L2 is a transcription factor in the Wnt signaling pathway affecting insulin secretion.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.4x increased T2D risk. Lifestyle intervention can significantly reduce risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~2x increased T2D risk. Active lifestyle management, glucose monitoring, and dietary modifications strongly recommended.

description: Variant near DRD2 dopamine receptor gene affecting receptor density. Associated with substance use disorders, reward processing, and impulsivity.
het_description: One copy: modestly reduced D2 receptor density. Slight increase in susceptibility to addictive behaviors.
hom_description: Two copies: ~30-40% reduced D2 receptor density. Increased risk for substance dependence and reward-seeking behavior.

description: Promoter variant affecting IL-6 expression. The C allele is associated with altered inflammatory responses and risk of cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and inflammatory conditions.
het_description: Intermediate IL-6 expression. Mildly altered inflammatory response.
hom_description: CC genotype: lower IL-6 levels at baseline but altered inflammatory regulation. May affect cardiovascular and metabolic risk.

description: Promoter variant increasing TNF-alpha production. Associated with susceptibility to autoimmune diseases, septic shock, and inflammatory conditions.
het_description: Increased TNF-alpha production. Elevated risk of autoimmune conditions and stronger inflammatory responses.
hom_description: Significantly increased TNF-alpha production. Higher risk of rheumatoid arthritis, IBD, and other autoimmune conditions.

description: First identified obesity susceptibility gene. FTO affects appetite regulation and energy expenditure. Each risk allele adds ~1.5 kg body weight on average.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.3x obesity risk. ~1.5 kg higher average body weight. Regular exercise can fully attenuate genetic risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.7x obesity risk. ~3 kg higher average body weight. Physical activity and dietary management particularly important.

description: Variant in insulin-like growth factor 2 binding protein affecting insulin secretion and beta-cell function.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.14x increased T2D risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.3x increased T2D risk. Modest contribution to overall diabetes susceptibility.

description: Variant near cell cycle regulators CDKN2A and CDKN2B affecting pancreatic beta-cell proliferation and insulin secretion.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.2x increased T2D risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.4x increased T2D risk.

description: The 9p21 locus is the strongest and most replicated genetic risk factor for coronary artery disease, independent of traditional risk factors. Affects vascular smooth muscle cell regulation.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.3x increased CAD risk. Standard cardiovascular risk management recommended.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.6x increased CAD risk. Enhanced cardiovascular screening and risk factor management recommended.

description: Additional 9p21 variant associated with myocardial infarction risk. Independent of lipid levels, blood pressure, and diabetes status.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.25x increased MI risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.6x increased MI risk. Comprehensive cardiovascular risk assessment recommended.

description: Strongest common genetic risk factor for atrial fibrillation. Located near PITX2, which is essential for left-right heart asymmetry and pulmonary vein development.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.7x increased AF risk. Monitor for palpitations, especially with advancing age.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~2.9x increased AF risk. Regular cardiac rhythm monitoring recommended, especially after age 50.

description: Variant in enhancer region near MYC oncogene. Associated with colorectal and prostate cancer risk. Affects Wnt signaling pathway regulation.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.2x increased colorectal cancer risk. Adhere to recommended screening schedules.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.5x increased colorectal and prostate cancer risk. Consider earlier initiation of screening.

description: Common variant in FGFR2 associated with estrogen receptor-positive breast cancer. FGFR2 plays a role in mammary gland development and cell proliferation.
het_description: One risk allele: ~1.26x increased breast cancer risk.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~1.6x increased breast cancer risk. Regular mammographic screening recommended.

description: Variant in mitochondrial superoxide dismutase affecting antioxidant defense. The Val allele reduces mitochondrial import efficiency, affecting reactive oxygen species neutralization.
het_description: Intermediate SOD2 activity. Normal oxidative stress management for most individuals.
hom_description: Val/Val: reduced SOD2 activity. May benefit from dietary antioxidants. Associated with modestly increased cancer and cardiovascular risk.

description: Variant causing rapid alcohol metabolism, leading to acetaldehyde accumulation and unpleasant flushing reaction. Strongly protective against alcohol dependence.
het_description: Faster alcohol metabolism. Mild flushing with alcohol. Protective against alcohol dependence.
hom_description: Very rapid alcohol metabolism. Strong flushing response. Strongly protective against alcoholism. Common in East Asian populations.

variant_name: ABCC11 earwax type / breast cancer risk
description: Variant affecting apocrine gland secretion. The C allele (wet earwax) is associated with slightly increased breast cancer risk and stronger body odor. T allele (dry earwax) is protective.
het_description: Wet earwax type. Normal apocrine secretion. Marginal breast cancer risk.
hom_description: CC (wet earwax): slightly increased breast cancer risk. TT (dry earwax): reduced risk and minimal body odor.

description: Regulatory variant controlling LCT gene expression. The A allele maintains lactase production into adulthood (lactase persistence). G/G genotype leads to lactose intolerance.
het_description: Lactase persistent. Can digest lactose normally into adulthood.
hom_description: GG: lactose intolerant (ancestral state). Lactase production declines after weaning. Avoid dairy or use lactase supplements. AA: lactase persistent.

description: Primary determinant of blue vs brown eye color. This HERC2 variant regulates OCA2 expression. GG strongly predicts blue eyes; AA predicts brown eyes.
het_description: AG: green, hazel, or intermediate eye color. Variable expression.
hom_description: GG: ~72% chance of blue eyes. AA: ~95% chance of brown eyes.

condition: Red Hair, Fair Skin, Melanoma Risk
description: Major MC1R variant associated with red hair, fair skin, freckling, and increased melanoma risk. Shifts melanin production from eumelanin to pheomelanin.
het_description: One copy: may have reddish tinge, fair skin, freckling. Modestly increased melanoma risk (~1.5x).
hom_description: Two copies: likely red hair, very fair skin, heavy freckling. ~3-4x increased melanoma risk. Strict sun protection essential.

description: The 'speed gene' variant. CC genotype produces alpha-actinin-3 in fast-twitch muscle fibers (sprint/power advantage). TT genotype lacks this protein (endurance advantage).
het_description: CT: intermediate muscle fiber composition. Mixed athletic profile.
hom_description: CC: full alpha-actinin-3 expression. Sprint/power advantage. TT: no alpha-actinin-3. Endurance advantage. ~18% of population.

description: Functional variant affecting dopamine breakdown rate. Val (G) = 'Warrior' (faster dopamine clearance, stress resilient). Met (A) = 'Worrier' (slower clearance, better cognitive performance under low stress).
het_description: Val/Met: intermediate dopamine metabolism. Balanced stress response and cognitive function.
hom_description: Val/Val: rapid dopamine clearance. Better stress resilience but lower baseline cognitive performance. Met/Met: slow dopamine clearance. Superior working memory but more stress-sensitive.

description: Variant in alpha-adducin affecting renal sodium reabsorption. The Trp460 allele is associated with salt-sensitive hypertension and enhanced response to diuretics.
het_description: Intermediate salt sensitivity. Moderate sodium restriction may benefit blood pressure control.
hom_description: Trp/Trp: salt-sensitive hypertension. Sodium restriction particularly effective. Enhanced response to thiazide diuretics.

description: Variant in apolipoprotein A5 promoter associated with elevated triglyceride levels and cardiovascular disease risk.
het_description: One risk allele: ~15-20% higher triglycerides. Dietary fat modification recommended.
hom_description: Two risk alleles: ~30-40% higher triglycerides. Increased cardiovascular risk. Omega-3 supplementation and dietary management recommended.

description: Promoter variant affecting IL-10 anti-inflammatory cytokine production. The A allele reduces IL-10 expression, potentially increasing susceptibility to inflammatory and autoimmune conditions.
het_description: Intermediate IL-10 production. Mildly altered anti-inflammatory response.
hom_description: AA: low IL-10 production. May have stronger inflammatory responses and slightly increased autoimmune susceptibility.

description: Variant in glutathione S-transferase Pi affecting detoxification of environmental carcinogens and reactive oxygen species.
het_description: Intermediate detoxification capacity. May benefit from dietary cruciferous vegetables and antioxidants.
hom_description: Val/Val: reduced detoxification capacity for certain carcinogens. Increased susceptibility to some environmentally-triggered cancers.
